articles.full_text:
Mumbai Weather Today: IMD issues yellow alert as maximum temperature may reach 36 degree Celsius
Severe heatwave was recorded in Mumbai last week, too. The city saw a bit of relief after the temperature fell close to 34 degrees Celsius on Wednesday. The weather department has also predicted dry weather and clear skies in Mumbai.
Mumbai: Amid scorching heat in Mumbai, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a yellow alert warning for the city and other parts of Maharashtra. As per the weather department, the city may witness the maximum temperature go up to 36 degree celsius
IMD urges locals to avoid going out in sun in afternoon
Severe heatwave was recorded in Mumbai last week, too. The city saw a bit of relief after the temperature fell close to 34 degrees Celsius on Wednesday. The weather department has also predicted dry weather and clear skies in Mumbai.
The IMD has also issued advice to avoid going directly into the sun during the afternoon hours. It also urged those working out to take a lot of water. Notably, the highest temperature of 41 degrees Celsius was recorded in Mumbai on May 12, 1979.
The weather department issues yellow alert
Apart from Mumbai, the IMD has also issued a yellow alert in Thane, Raigad, and Palghar starting today. Among other parts of Maharashtra, Thane may also witness maximum temperature to go up to 38 degrees Celsius. The heat intensity is also expected in Raigad and Thane. Notably, Thane is expected to receive light rain and thunderstorms after May 18.
“Heat wave conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets in the districts of North Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada. Hot day and humid conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets in the districts of Konkan -Goa,” Regional Meteorological Center, Mumbai wrote on X.
Importantly, Buldhana, which was once considered a cool place, is also witnessing a sharp increase in temperatures. While the temperature used to remain between 38 and 39 degrees Celsius, it has increased 43 degrees Celsius. According to experts, the increase in temperature is likely to impact on human health, crops and livestock.
Click for more latest Mumbai news. Also get top headlines and latest news from India and around the world at News9.
